papers.title: Transparency as Architecture: Structural Compliance Gaps in EU AI Act Article 50 II
papers.innovation: Analyzes fundamental architectural and technical constraints that collide with the EU AI Act's Article 50 II mandates for dual transparency in AI-generated content, offering critical insights into regulatory compliance challenges for AI engineers.
papers.use_case: Guiding the architectural design and implementation of AI systems to ensure compliance with emerging regulations like the EU AI Act, particularly for generative AI and content labeling, thereby enabling responsible AI deployment.

papers.title: Concerning Uncertainty -- A Systematic Survey of Uncertainty-Aware XAI
papers.innovation: Provides a comprehensive systematic survey and categorization of existing methods for incorporating uncertainty into Explainable AI (XAI) pipelines. It synthesizes approaches like Bayesian and Monte Carlo for UAXAI and their evaluation, offering a structured overview for practitioners.
papers.use_case: For AI engineers implementing XAI, this paper serves as a vital guide to selecting and evaluating appropriate uncertainty-aware explanation methods, enhancing the trustworthiness, reliability, and robustness of AI systems, especially in high-stakes applications.

papers.title: Compliance-Aware Predictive Process Monitoring: A Neuro-Symbolic Approach
papers.innovation: Introduces a neuro-symbolic approach for predictive process monitoring that inherently considers and enforces compliance rules. This method combines data-driven learning with symbolic domain knowledge to create models that are not only predictive but also compliant and interpretable.
papers.benchmarks: Implied improvements in compliance adherence, robustness, and potentially enhanced predictive accuracy and interpretability, particularly in scenarios where explicit process rules or regulations apply.
papers.use_case: Developing predictive AI systems for domains with strict regulatory or operational compliance requirements, such as fraud detection, healthcare monitoring, and business process management, where logical rules must be followed and verified.
